title.zh: 英语从句到底怎么回事

summary.en: A clause is a whole sentence that has become a single component inside a bigger sentence. That one idea explains every type of subordinate clause.
summary.zh: 从句就是一个本可独立成句的句子，变成了更大句子里的一个成分。理解了这一点，所有从句的分类就迎刃而解。

heading.zh: 从句为何让中文母语者困惑

body.en: Chinese has expressions that match English clauses, but the sentence structure is often very different — the way an adjective (relative) clause is built barely exists in Chinese. On top of that, the terminology is a mess: one concept goes by several names, and names from different levels get mistaken for siblings. Start from the underlying principle instead.
body.zh: 中文虽然有和英语从句对应的表达，但语句结构往往很不一样——形容词从句的构成方式在中文里几乎不存在。再加上术语繁多：同一个概念有好几个名字，不同层次的名字又容易被当成同级概念。所以要从原理入手。

concepts.sourceRef: 但是往往在语句结构上很不一样

heading.en: The principle: a sentence becomes one component
heading.zh: 原理：一个句子充当一个成分

body.en: Take 'The rabbit ate a carrot.' Now wrap it: 'I saw that the rabbit ate a carrot.' What did I see? Not the rabbit, not the carrot, but the whole event 'the rabbit ate a carrot'. That entire former sentence has become one big object. A sentence A can serve as a component of a larger sentence B; A then depends on B, obeys B, and can no longer stand alone.
body.zh: 拿“兔子吃了根胡萝卜”来说，套进去就成了“我看见这兔子吃了根胡萝卜”。我看见的是什么？不是兔子，也不是胡萝卜，而是“兔子吃了根胡萝卜”这整个事件。这个原本能独立成句的句子，成了一个大宾语。一个句子 A 可以充当更大句子 B 的某个成分；A 从此附属于 B、服从 B，也不能再独立成句了。

concepts.sourceRef: 其实是“兔子吃了根胡萝卜”这个动作或者事件本身啊

body.en: The embedded sentence A is the subordinate clause; the rest, B, that hosts it is the main clause. Together they form one complex sentence. Note: '从' in 从句 means 'subordinate / dependent', not 'the back half' — a subordinate clause is not simply the second half of a sentence.
body.zh: 被嵌入的句子 A 是从句（subordinate clause）；容纳它的其余部分 B 是主句（main clause）。两者合起来是一句复杂句。注意：“从句”的“从”是附属、服从的意思，不是“后半段”——从句并不等于句子的后半部分。

concepts.sourceRef: A就是所谓从句，subordinate clause，B就是所谓主句，main clause

heading.en: Three sentence types: simple, compound, complex
heading.zh: 三种句子：简单句、复合句、复杂句

body.en: A simple sentence can't be split further without losing its meaning. Join simple sentences with a conjunction as equals — neither subordinate — and you get a compound sentence. When one is the main clause and the other a subordinate clause filling a component slot, you get a complex sentence. (Domestic books often translate compound as '并列句' and complex as '复合句'; to avoid confusion call them '并列复合句' and '主从复合句'.)
body.zh: 简单句就是不能再拆、否则句意不完整的句子。用连词把简单句不分主次地连起来，就是复合句（compound）。当一个是主句、另一个是充当成分的从句时，就是复杂句（complex）。（国内不少书把 compound 译作“并列句”、complex 译作“复合句”；为免混淆，可分别叫“并列复合句”和“主从复合句”。）

concepts.sourceRef: 英语有三种句子，一，简单句，simple sentences，二，复合句，compound sentences，三，复杂句

heading.zh: 按句子成分给从句分类

body.en: Every clause fills a component slot, so list the slots and you have listed the clause types. Except the predicate verb (the core, which can never be a clause), each component can be a clause: subject clause, object clause, object-complement clause, subject-complement (predicative) clause, attributive (relative) clause, adverbial clause, and appositive clause.
body.zh: 每个从句都充当某个成分，所以把成分列出来，从句的种类也就列出来了。除了最特殊的谓语动词（核心，永远不能由从句充当）之外，每种成分都能由从句充当：主语从句、宾语从句、宾语补语从句、主语补语从句（即表语从句）、定语从句、状语从句、同位语从句。

concepts.sourceRef: 每一种句子成分其实都可以由从句充当

body.en: Clauses can also be grouped by the part of speech they act like. Subject, object, subject-complement (predicative), object-complement and appositive clauses all behave like a big noun, so they are noun clauses. The attributive clause behaves like a big adjective — hence adjective clause, also called relative clause (it uses relative words). The adverbial clause behaves like a big adverb — an adverb clause, splitting into time, place, condition, reason, manner, comparison, and so on.
body.zh: 从句也能按它们相当于的词类来分。主语从句、宾语从句、主语补语从句（表语从句）、宾语补语从句、同位语从句都相当于一个大名词，合称名词性从句。定语从句相当于一个大形容词，所以又叫形容词从句，也叫关系从句（因为用到关系词）。状语从句相当于一个大副词，叫副词从句，又细分为时间、地点、条件、原因、方式、比较等类型。

concepts.sourceRef: 定语从句、形容词从句、关系从句是一回事

note.en: A complete simple sentence on its own — subject + verb + object.
note.zh: 一个完整的简单句——主语 + 谓语动词 + 宾语。

examples.text: I saw that the rabbit ate a carrot.

note.en: The whole former sentence 'the rabbit ate a carrot' is now one big object — an object clause. 'I saw' is the main clause.
note.zh: 原本的整句“the rabbit ate a carrot”现在成了一个大宾语——宾语从句。“I saw”是主句。

examples.text: The rabbit was hungry, so it ate a carrot.

note.en: Two equal simple sentences joined by 'so' — neither is subordinate. This is a compound sentence, not a complex one.
note.zh: 两个对等的简单句用“so”连接——谁也不附属于谁。这是复合句（并列），不是复杂句。

examples.text: I know the rabbit that ate a carrot.

note.en: Here the clause modifies 'the rabbit' like a big adjective — an attributive (relative) clause, using the relative word 'that'.
note.zh: 这里的从句像一个大形容词修饰“the rabbit”——定语（关系）从句，用了关系词“that”。

examples.text: What the rabbit ate was a carrot.

note.en: The clause 'What the rabbit ate' acts like a big noun in the subject slot — a subject (noun) clause.
note.zh: 从句“What the rabbit ate”像一个大名词占据主语位置——主语从句（名词性从句）。

prompt.en: In 'I saw that the rabbit ate a carrot', what role does 'that the rabbit ate a carrot' play?
prompt.zh: 在“I saw that the rabbit ate a carrot”中，“that the rabbit ate a carrot”充当什么？

explain.en: What I saw is the whole event — the entire former sentence becomes one big object, i.e. an object clause.
explain.zh: 我看见的是整个事件——原本的整句变成一个大宾语，即宾语从句。

prompt.en: Which sentence is a complex sentence (main clause + subordinate clause)?
prompt.zh: 哪一句是复杂句（主句 + 从句）？

text.en: The rabbit was hungry, so it ate a carrot.
text.zh: The rabbit was hungry, so it ate a carrot.

text.en: I know the rabbit that ate a carrot.
text.zh: I know the rabbit that ate a carrot.

explain.en: B has a subordinate (relative) clause filling a slot. A joins two equals (compound); C is a simple sentence.
explain.zh: B 含一个充当成分的定语从句。A 是对等连接（复合句）；C 是简单句。

prompt.en: A clause that behaves like a big adjective and modifies a noun is called an ____ clause (also: relative clause).
prompt.zh: 相当于一个大形容词、修饰名词的从句叫作____从句（也叫关系从句）。

explain.en: Attributive clause = adjective clause = relative clause — three names, one concept.
explain.zh: 定语从句 = 形容词从句 = 关系从句——同一概念的三个名字。

prompt.en: Subject, object, predicative and appositive clauses all act like a big noun, so together they are called ____ clauses.
prompt.zh: 主语、宾语、表语、同位语从句都相当于一个大名词，所以合称____从句。

explain.en: Clauses that behave like a noun are grouped as noun (nominal) clauses.
explain.zh: 相当于名词的从句合称名词性（名词）从句。

quizzes.sentence: A subordinate clause can stand alone as a complete sentence.
quizzes.fix: A subordinate clause cannot stand alone as a complete sentence.

explain.en: Once a sentence becomes a component of a bigger one, it depends on the main clause and can no longer stand alone.
explain.zh: 一旦一个句子变成更大句子的成分，它就附属于主句，不能再独立成句了。

prompt.en: Which component can NEVER be filled by a clause?
prompt.zh: 哪种句子成分永远不能由从句充当？

explain.en: The predicate verb is the special core component; every other slot can be a clause, but the predicate verb cannot.
explain.zh: 谓语动词是最特殊的核心成分；其余每种成分都能由从句充当，唯独谓语动词不行。

note.en: Start with a complete simple sentence.
note.zh: 先从一个完整的简单句开始。

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: I saw that the rabbit ate a carrot.

note.en: Now it is a big object — an object clause.
note.zh: 现在它成了一个大宾语——宾语从句。

lines.speaker: Teacher
lines.text: What the rabbit ate was a carrot.

note.en: Same event, now in the subject slot — a subject (noun) clause.
note.zh: 同一事件，这次占据主语位置——主语从句（名词性从句）。

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: I know the rabbit that ate a carrot.

note.en: And now it modifies a noun — an attributive (relative) clause.
note.zh: 这次它修饰名词——定语（关系）从句。
